content.problemStatement:
Alice and Bob are walking on an infinite straight street. Initially, both are at the position X=0X=0X=0 and they start walking in the direction of increasing XXX. After NNN seconds, they stop. Let's denote Alice's speed and Bob's speed during the iii-th of these seconds by AiA_iAi​ and BiB_iBi​ respectively.

Sometimes, Alice and Bob walk together, i.e. with the same speed side by side. Let's define the weird distance as the total distance they walk this way. Find this weird distance.
constraints: Sum of N over all test cases does not exceed 1000000
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case: first line contains integer N, the number of seconds. The second line contains N space‑separated integers A1 … AN – Alice's speed in each second. The third line contains N space‑separated integers B1 … BN – Bob's speed in each second.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line with one integer – the total weird distance, i.e. the sum of speeds for all seconds where Alice and Bob walked side by side (Ai = Bi).

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: equal speeds at seconds 1 (speed 1) and 4 (speed 4) → 1+4=5.\nTest case 2: no second has equal speeds → 0.\nTest case 3: both seconds have equal speeds 3 → 3+3=6.

solution.approach: For each test case iterate i from 1 to N. Keep a 64‑bit accumulator ans = 0. If Ai equals Bi, add Ai (or Bi) to ans. After processing all seconds output ans. The algorithm uses O(N) time and O(1) extra space per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, where N is the length of the arrays. Overall O(total N) ≤ 10^6.
